## Summary

Replaces ambiguous dashboard status labels with agent-readiness vocabulary, adds calibration/published-work detection, and protects the platform from "but this famous book scored 7.2" criticism.

### Status vocabulary by evaluation purpose

**Normal unpublished manuscripts:**
| System state | Author-facing label |
|---|---|
| complete + score ≥ 8.0 | Agent ready |
| complete + score ≥ 7.5 | Near ready |
| complete + score ≥ 6.5 | Improving |
| complete + score < 6.5 | Not agent ready |
| running/queued | In progress |
| failed | Evaluation failed |

**Calibration / test runs** (detected by title containing TEST FILE, CALIBRATION, BENCHMARK, etc.):
| System state | Author-facing label |
|---|---|
| complete (any score) | Calibration complete |
| running | Calibration in progress |
| failed | Calibration failed |

**Published / reference works** (future: detected by upload-form purpose selector):
| System state | Author-facing label |
|---|---|
| complete (any score) | Reference eval complete |
| running | Reference eval in progress |

### Other changes

- **Report disclaimer:** Blue "Published Work Context" banner on report pages for calibration/benchmark works: *"RevisionGrade is evaluating the submitted text against its manuscript-readiness criteria, not against the work's historical importance, sales record, cultural influence, or existing reputation."*
- **Tooltip on every status badge** explaining what the status means
- **KPI cards:** "Market readiness" → "Agent readiness", "Ready for review" → "Agent ready"
- **Dashboard footnote:** Added *"Scores reflect alignment with contemporary agent-submission standards, not literary merit. Published classics may score differently."*
- **"Build Agent Readiness Package" hidden** for calibration/published-reference runs
- **CSS:** New `.rg-status--not-ready`, `.rg-status--calibration`, `.rg-status--reference` badge styles

### Follow-up (not in this PR)

The permanent fix is an **upload-form purpose selector** so users can declare:
- ○ My unpublished manuscript
- ○ A published book / classic / bestseller
- ○ A public-domain calibration text
- ○ A test file
- ○ An excerpt / chapter

This would replace title-pattern detection with a first-class `evaluation_purpose` field in the data model.

## Scope

**Touches:** Dashboard evaluation table, dashboard KPI cards, dashboard footnote, report page header, CSS
**Does NOT touch:** Scoring logic, readiness threshold, evaluation pipeline, extraction, or any data model

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Title-pattern detection is a heuristic — works for explicit labels like "(TEST FILE)" but won't catch someone uploading "Pride and Prejudice" without marking it. The upload-form selector is the real fix (follow-up).
- "Not agent ready" is more honest than "Below standard" but still direct. Tooltip softens with full context.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — no extraction, scoring, or pipeline code modified. UI/copy only.
